authority_boundary.scope: macro-side reconstruction handoff language and projection-readiness checks

public_substrate_rule.statement: The public microcosm releases public substrate organs and code patterns wherever projection or refactor is safe.
public_substrate_includes: public-safe macro proof bodies with provenance
public-safe macro tool bodies with provenance
public-safe macro pattern bodies with provenance
public-safe macro receipt bodies with provenance
route-plane, WorkItem, Work Ledger, and evidence-cell contracts
paper-module authoring contracts and public-safe doctrine sections
not_excluded_by_default: public-safe body imports after scanner classification, provenance recording, validation refs, and claim ceilings

allowed_when: material_class is public_macro_pattern_body, public_macro_tool_body, public_macro_receipt_body, or public_macro_proof_body
private_state_risk routes to direct_public or public_safe_with_light_edits
source refs, provenance refs, validation refs, applied edits when needed, and claim ceiling are present
scanner rejects no true private sentinel or forbidden material class
release, publication, recipient-work, private-root equivalence, and whole-system correctness claims remain false
blocked_when: material is raw seed, operator thread, provider payload, credential, secret, recipient packet, release packet, live operator state, or non-public evidence body
public-safe import claims source authority above the macro contract
public_safe_macro_import_rule.lean_policy: Lean and proof bodies use the same public-safe macro import classification as tools, patterns, and receipts; they are neither privileged nor excluded as a special case.

excluded_classes.label: forbidden content body
examples: provider or oracle private proof body text
excluded_classes.public_treatment: exclude, replace with synthetic replacement fixture, or reduce to schema-only projection

excluded_classes.label: live operator state
excluded_classes.public_treatment: exclude or replace with synthetic replacement fixture

excluded_classes.label: true private source body
examples: formal/prover lab payload body that contains provider or oracle private material
excluded_classes.public_treatment: path-level lineage, body-redacted digest, synthetic replacement fixture, schema-only projection, or case review; do not use this class for public-safe macro proof/tool/pattern/receipt bodies

excluded_classes.label: non-public evidence body
examples: evidence used for reconstruction lineage but not copied into fixtures
excluded_classes.public_treatment: omit body; carry lineage, schema, anti-claim, or replacement fixture only

projection_modes.direct_public_with_provenance: Allow a public-safe macro body to flow after scanner classification, source refs, provenance refs, validation refs, and claim ceiling are present.
projection_modes.public_safe_body_with_light_edits: Allow a none/low-risk public-safe body after private path/reference cleanup, provenance, validation refs, and bounded claims.
projection_modes.case_review: Hold medium-risk material until a typed review receipt chooses direct import, redaction, schema-only, or synthetic-only treatment.
projection_modes.public_safe_projection: Project or refactor structure only after blocklist and authority checks pass.
projection_modes.synthetic_replacement_fixture: Replace a forbidden content body or live operator state with toy data that exercises the same contract.
projection_modes.schema_only_projection: Expose field names, shapes, validation rules, and error codes without source rows or payload bodies.
projection_modes.path_level_lineage_only: Carry path, id, digest, status, or body-redacted receipt metadata.
projection_modes.target_only_not_source: Name a public substrate path as a future destination without treating that path as source authority.
projection_modes.forbidden_content_body_excluded: Reject the source as fixture input, receipt body, implementation evidence, or authority.

legacy_term_translation.private_state_scan: compatibility validator label for non-public content body, forbidden content body, private source body, and live operator state scanning
legacy_term_translation.private_state_risks: compatibility risk-field label for non-public content body, forbidden content body, private source body, and live operator state classification
legacy_term_translation.private_boundary: compatibility failure/status label for an authority boundary failure involving a forbidden content body, private source body, non-public evidence body, live operator state, or public-root mutation/source-authority attempt
legacy_term_translation.blocked_private_body_reference: compatibility dry-run status for a blocked forbidden content body, private source body, non-public evidence body, or live operator state reference
legacy_term_translation.hidden_substrate_integration_manifest: legacy source-ledger label for an authority-boundary source-lineage manifest over non-public evidence bodies
legacy_term_translation.hidden-substrate: legacy source-ledger adjective for non-public evidence-body lineage, not public substrate exclusion
legacy_term_translation.sanitization: legacy source-ledger label for replacing forbidden content bodies with synthetic replacement fixtures or schema-only projections
legacy_term_translation.sanitized: legacy source-ledger label for forbidden content-body replacement, not substrate cleanup
legacy_term_translation.private_only: legacy source-ledger decision label for non-public evidence-body omission or direct public leaf exclusion
legacy_term_translation.private-only: legacy source-ledger decision label for non-public evidence-body omission or direct public leaf exclusion

macro_only_compatibility_policy.keep_private_forever: Compatibility projection-mode label only. It means forbidden content-body exclusion from fixture bodies, receipt bodies, source authority, or implementation evidence because the source class is a forbidden content body, non-public evidence body, private source body, live operator state, or release/dissemination wrapper. It does not mean public substrate exclusion.
macro_only_compatibility_policy.macro_only: Compatibility route label only. It means macro-side planning evidence, non-public evidence-body omission, schema-only projection, or direct public leaf exclusion. It does not mark public substrate organs or code patterns as private.
macro_only_compatibility_policy.macro-only: Compatibility disposition label only. It means the row is not a standalone public leaf driver because its body is non-public evidence, macro-side planning evidence, or schema-only material. It does not block public-safe projection of releasable public substrate organs.
macro_only_compatibility_policy.macro_only_evidence_not_candidate_leaf: Compatibility overlay role only. It means the row remains evidence for lineage or anti-claim policy while its body is omitted from public fixtures and it is excluded from direct leaf scheduling.
macro_only_compatibility_policy.macro_only_schema_exclusion_contract: Compatibility fixture/negative-case label only. It means a macro-only row with an attached private payload body must be rejected or reduced to schema-only projection.
macro_only_compatibility_policy.macro_only_boundary: Compatibility normalization flag only. It means an authority boundary around non-public evidence bodies, forbidden content bodies, private source bodies, or live operator state.
macro_only_compatibility_policy.macro_planning_only: Compatibility blocklist projection policy only. It means planning references may remain in macro reconstruction artifacts while public fixture bodies and implementation imports are forbidden.

compatibility_label_policy.private_state_scan: Compatibility validator/file label only. It means scan for non-public content bodies, forbidden content bodies, private source bodies, and live operator state before accepting a public fixture or receipt.
compatibility_label_policy.private_state_scan_result: Compatibility receipt field only. It must record body-exclusion results and must not imply that the public substrate is private.
compatibility_label_policy.private_state_scan_focus: Compatibility negative-case field only. It names the non-public content bodies, forbidden content bodies, private source bodies, or live operator state classes a fixture must reject.
compatibility_label_policy.private_state_scan_summary: Compatibility receipt summary only. It summarizes body/state exclusion results without carrying source bodies.
compatibility_label_policy.private_state_risk: Compatibility risk field only. It flags content/state hazards that require public-safe projection, synthetic replacement fixtures, schema-only projection, or omission.
compatibility_label_policy.private_state_risks: Compatibility risk array only. It classifies non-public content bodies, forbidden content bodies, private source bodies, or live operator state hazards before projection.
compatibility_label_policy.private_state_risk_summary: Compatibility risk summary only. It describes why a public-safe projection needs omission, schema-only projection, or synthetic replacement fixtures.
compatibility_label_policy.private_state_forbidden_terms: Compatibility fixture filename only. It carries scanner vocabulary, not source authority or public substrate exclusion.

hyphenated_private_state_label_policy.private-state scan: Compatibility prose only. It means scanner coverage for non-public content bodies, forbidden content bodies, private source bodies, and live operator state.
hyphenated_private_state_label_policy.private-state exclusions: Compatibility prose only. It means non-public content and live-state exclusions, not substrate exclusion.
hyphenated_private_state_label_policy.private-state bodies: Compatibility prose only. It means private source bodies, forbidden content bodies, or live operator state bodies.
hyphenated_private_state_label_policy.private-root gate: Compatibility prose only. It means authority-boundary gate over forbidden content/state sources, not a private substrate gate.
hyphenated_private_state_label_policy.private-state authority leak: Compatibility prose only. It means a forbidden content body, private source body, or live operator state was treated as public authority.
hyphenated_private_state_label_policy.current_public_root_guard: When microcosm-substrate/** appears in private_state_exclusions compatibility arrays, it excludes current public-root source authority or public-root mutation attempts during macro validation; it does not make the future public substrate private.

private_boundary_failure_label_policy.summary: Compatibility failure/status labels mean authority-boundary failures against forbidden content bodies, private source bodies, non-public evidence bodies, live operator state, or public-root mutation/source-authority attempts; they do not mark the public substrate as private.
private_boundary_failure_label_policy.private_boundary: Compatibility failure class only. It means an authority boundary rejected a forbidden content body, private source body, non-public evidence body, live operator state, or public-root mutation/source-authority attempt.
private_boundary_failure_label_policy.private_boundary_schema_or_synthetic_onl: Compatibility overlay flag only. It means the row can remain as schema-only projection or synthetic replacement fixture input after body-bearing evidence is omitted.
private_boundary_failure_label_policy.private_boundary_order_violation: Compatibility dependency-order failure only. It means an authority boundary validator or standard was ordered too late to guard forbidden content/state input.
private_boundary_failure_label_policy.blocked_private_body_reference: Compatibility dry-run status only. It means a forbidden content body, private source body, non-public evidence body, or live operator state reference was blocked.
private_boundary_failure_label_policy.blocked_private_boundary_violation: Compatibility expected error code only. It means the validator rejected an authority-boundary violation involving forbidden content/state input.
private_boundary_failure_label_policy.blocked_private_state: Compatibility blocker/status label only. It means non-public content bodies, forbidden content bodies, private source bodies, or live operator state were blocked before receipt or fixture acceptance.
private_boundary_failure_label_policy.private_body_reference_detected: Compatibility finding label only. It means a body-bearing non-public source reference was detected and must be omitted or replaced.
private_boundary_failure_label_policy.private_state_body_ref: Compatibility fixture/receipt field label only. It points to a forbidden body reference that must not be copied.
private_boundary_failure_label_policy.private_state_body_ref_blocks: Compatibility blocklist label only. It names forbidden content body, private source body, non-public evidence body, or live operator state references that block direct projection.
private_boundary_failure_label_policy.private_state_body_or_operator_voice_bod: Compatibility fixture class only. It means private source body or live operator state body material that must be replaced by a synthetic replacement fixture or schema-only projection.
private_boundary_failure_label_policy.private_state_body_leak: Compatibility fixture variant only. It is a synthetic negative-case filename for forbidden content/state leakage, not an observed source body.
private_boundary_failure_label_policy.PRIVATE_STATE_BODY_IN_FRESHNESS_RECEIPT: Compatibility error code only. It means a freshness receipt attempted to carry a forbidden content body, private source body, non-public evidence body, or live operator state body.

source_ledger_legacy_label_policy.summary: Legacy source-ledger labels remain evidence labels only. They must be translated before build use and do not mark the public substrate as hidden or private.
source_ledger_legacy_label_policy.hidden_substrate_integration_manifest: Legacy pattern id. Translate to authority-boundary source-lineage manifest for non-public evidence bodies.
source_ledger_legacy_label_policy.hidden-substrate: Legacy title adjective. Translate to source-lineage over forbidden content bodies, private source bodies, non-public evidence bodies, or live operator state; do not use it as public substrate posture.
source_ledger_legacy_label_policy.sanitization: Legacy process label. Translate to replacing forbidden content bodies with a synthetic replacement fixture or schema-only projection.
source_ledger_legacy_label_policy.sanitized: Legacy process label. Translate to forbidden content-body replacement or schema-only projection, not substrate cleanup.
source_ledger_legacy_label_policy.private_only: Legacy decision label. Translate to non-public evidence-body omission, schema-only projection, or direct public leaf exclusion.
source_ledger_legacy_label_policy.private-only: Legacy decision label. Translate to non-public evidence-body omission, schema-only projection, or direct public leaf exclusion.

discouraged_terms.replacement: forbidden content body, private source body, or non-public evidence body
exclude forbidden content bodies and live operator state
public substrate organ with forbidden content bodies omitted, or non-public evidence-only row
non-public evidence-body omission, schema-only projection, or macro-side planning evidence
replace forbidden content with a synthetic replacement fixture
private source bodies, forbidden content bodies, or live operator state bodies

language_gate.policy: Banned phrases may appear only inside this policy's discouraged_terms or term-replacement catalog, not in key public build handoff or projection-planning surfaces. The checker must also fail when this list drifts from the actual scanned surfaces, when mechanical build-driver artifacts lack this policy reference, or when compatibility private-state labels, hyphenated private-state prose, macro-only labels, private-boundary failure/status labels, or source-ledger legacy labels are not translated as non-public content/body and live-state scanners, risk classifiers, authority-boundary guards, non-public evidence-body omissions, schema-only projections, macro-side planning evidence, forbidden content-body exclusions, direct-leaf exclusions, authority-boundary failures, source-lineage manifests, synthetic replacement fixtures, or schema-only projections.

anti_claim: This policy hardens boundary language only; it does not build public code, authorize public-root writes, publish release material, or prove public build readiness beyond the checker receipt.
